title: Qlog Import And Rehydration Verification
scope: Verify the sibling qlog import package, contained JSON hydration, sequential JSON Text Sequences hydration, retained-model preservation, string and Stream entry points, and rejection of unsupported or malformed input. The slice does not include replay execution or transport simulation.
verification_method: Requirement-homed unit tests, round-trip comparisons against repository-produced serializer output, and negative tests for malformed and unsupported inputs.
preconditions: The qlog SpecTrace artifacts validate successfully under the repository's JSON validation script.
The contained JSON and sequential serializer outputs are available for round-trip comparison.
The sibling import package exists and exposes the planned import entry points.
procedure: Run scripts/Validate-SpecTraceJson.ps1 over the qlog canonical artifacts.
Generate contained qlog JSON output from the existing serializer and hydrate it through the import surface.
Generate sequential JSON Text Sequences output from the existing serializer and hydrate it through the import surface.
Compare the hydrated models against the source models for file metadata, traces, events, common fields, vantage points, trace errors, and extension members.
Exercise invalid JSON, malformed sequential framing, and unsupported input shapes to confirm the importer fails cleanly.
Compare string and Stream import paths where both are exposed.
expected_result: The import package hydrates repository-produced qlog output back into the retained model without losing opaque data, and invalid or unsupported input is rejected clearly instead of being silently reshaped.
